content.name: Bachelor Paper: Comparison of data exfiltration techniques for stealing Windows using keystroke injection attacks
content.content: This paper compares keystroke injection data exfiltration techniques using specifically developed DuckyScript payloads deployed using the Hak5 USB Rubber Ducky. In particular, it covers exfiltration over a physical medium (USB), exfiltration over a network medium (HTTP), as well as the novel exfiltration method known as keystroke reflection. It was written as the final thesis of my Bachelor's degree at UAS Technikum Wien.

content.name: Write-Up: TryHackMe Red Team Capstone Challenge
content.content: The TryHackMe Red Team Capstone Challenge simulates a red team assessment on the corporate network infrastructure of a fictional bank. The objective was to enumerate and exploit the Active Directory environment to conduct a fraudulent money transfer to show the compromise of the bank's network. This 40+ page long write-up details the steps taken to complete the challenge and includes techniques like Kerberoasting, DCSync attacks, Golden ticket attacks as well as lateral movement and persistence inside an Active Directory domain.

content.name: parcel-logistics-service
content.content: The 'Parcel Logistics Service' application is a web-based parcel tracking system that allows users to track the status of their parcels based on real geographic data and coordinates. The route on which parcels are transferred is predicted automatically. It was developed as part of the 'Software Components Systems Lab' course at UAS Technikum Wien. The application is written in C# using the ASP.NET framework and uses a PostgreSQL database. It was developed in an agile manner using the Scrum methodology and Azure DevOps for continuous and test-driven development.

content.name: ml-bd-for-code-quality
content.content: As part of a 3 semester long innovation project, 'Machine Learning and Big Data for code quality' has been developed for comparing the efficiency and quality of different supervised and unsupervised machine learning algorithms for log file analysis. It clusters and groups stack traces based on their error type together and displays notable results and metrics to the user. The project is written in Python and uses the scikit-learn library for machine learning as well as the py-simplegui library to create a graphical user interface. It was developed in an agile manner using the Scrum methodology and git for version control.

content.name: personal-website
content.content: This website has been a personal dream of mine for a long time. It is a static website that is hosted on GitHub Pages and is written in pure HTML, CSS and JavaScript. It is used to showcase my skills and projects to potential employers and to provide a platform for me to share my knowledge and experience with others. It is also used as a blog to share my thoughts and opinions on various cybersecurity-related topics, write-ups and other insights. The project is my personal twist on a terminal-themed website that uses user-input to navigate through the website and its content. The website uses a JSON file on the backend to simulate the directory structure of a UNIX-based shell, which is parsed and rendered on the frontend using JavaScript. A priority of mine is to make the terminal feel as authentic as possible, which is why I have implemented custom CSS styling, Tab-autocompletion, as well as the a clear-screen mechanic. Futhermore, the website aims to be explored and enjoyed by the user, which is why I have implemented various easter eggs and hidden features that can be discovered when browsing. Of course, there also exists a non-terminal version of the website for users that prefer a more traditional experience.

content.name: CSharp
content.content: The CSharp programming language has been thoroughly used during my studies at UAS Technikum Wien. Among other projects, it was mainly used to develop large scale full-stack applications like the Parcel Logistics Service application, as well as the Tour Planner application. It was used in conjunction with relational database managements like PostgreSQL and MySQL as well as with platforms like Azure DevOps and GitHub to practice continuous and agile development.

content.name: Nim
content.content: Nim is a low-level systems programming language that combines Python-like syntax and a good developer experience with C-like performance. I started learning Nim in december of 2022 with the yearly Advent of Code challenge due to its similarity to Python and its ability to compile to C, C++ and JavaScript. I have since used Nim for various small projects like a Discord Bot, as well as picked up a passion for malware development due to it's fantastic integration with the Windows API. Most notably, I have developed a shellcode stager which is able to load and execute shellcode from a remote server into memory on the target machine, bypassing static defense and detection mechanisms.

content.name: Python
content.content: Python is used by me mainly for scripting and automating tasks and processes, as well as for writing exploits and tools for penetration testing engagements. It was also the language of choice for the ML-BD-for-code-quality machine learning project at UAS Technikum Wien.

content.name: C|C++
content.content: I learned C during my first semester at UAS Technikum Wien. It was used to teach the basics of programming and to introduce us to the C programming language. I have since used it for projects like a mail server application that showcases my ability to work with sockets and the TCP/IP protocol. Further, I have used it to practice my understanding of the Windows API, though I have since switched to Nim for this purpose. C++ on the other hand was taught at UAS Technikum Wien using algorithms and data structures as well as object oriented game-programming.

content.name: JavaScript|HTML|CSS|PHP
content.content: JavaScript has been used in combination with HTML, CSS and PHP during my studies at UAS Technikum Wien to develop web-applications. I am experienced with working with the LAMP technology stack, Node.js and the Angular web development framework. Personal projects using this language include a Discord bot that I have made for my friends and I to use, as well as this personal portfolio website, which features custom styling and a innovative JSON-based backend to simulate the look and feel of a UNIX-based terminal. I am also familiar with the OWASP Top 10 and identifying vulnerable JavaScript code that can be exploited by attackers.

content.name: SQL
content.content: Almost all projects I have worked on have included a database integration of some sorts. I have worked with MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB and Microsoft SQL Server. I am equipped with the ability to securely integrate databases into applications and to write complex queries to retrieve and manipulate data. As a penetration tester, I am also able to identify and exploit SQL injection vulnerabilities and report remediation advice.

content.name: Offshore
content.content: Offshore Pro Lab is an Active Directory lab that simulates the look and feel of a real-world corporate network. With this network, I was tasked to assess the external perimeter, gain an internal foothold and pivot across multiple hosts and forests. Users start from an external perspective and have to penetrate the “DMZ” and then move laterally through the CORP.LOCAL, DEV, ADMIN and CLIENT forests to complete the lab. To track progress, there are multiple flags planted along the way as well as a few side challenges that are not required to advance within the Active Directory environment. This lab proves the ability to compromise an real-world Active Directory environment, as well as the ability to pivot across multiple hosts and forests.

content.name: RastaLabs
content.content: RastaLabs Pro Lab is an Active Directory lab that simulates a red team engagement on a real-world corporate environment. The initial foothold is achieved by conducting a phishing attack on the company's employees. After gaining access to the network, the user has to escalate privileges and move laterally through the network to complete the lab. To track progress, there are multiple flags planted along the way as well as a few side challenges that are not required to advance within the Active Directory environment. Due to the installation of various endpoint security restrictions and anti-virus solutions deployed on the lab, RastaLabs requires the attacker to use advanced evasion and obfuscation techniques to move across the domain and compromise the Domain Admin accounts.

content.name: Zephyr
content.content: Zephyr Pro Lab is the newest HackTheBox professional lab environment on the market and simulates an Active Directory environment consistent of multiple domains and forests. The objective is to establish a foothold on the corporate network and move laterally and vertically to reach Domain Admin privileges. To track progress, there are multiple flags planted along the way as well as a few side challenges that are not required to advance within the Active Directory environment. Apart from phishing and password cracking, this lab primarily focuses on Active Directory exploitation, Kerberos attacks as well as SQL Server attacks.

content.name: README.md
content.content: There are a total of 3 secret commands available. Try to find them all! :)
